items.q: Why was Jenner's smallpox vaccination significant in 1796?
items.correct: He showed cowpox material could prevent smallpox safely — the first effective vaccine, though he did not know why it worked; germ theory came nearly a century later.
items.misconception: Jenner discovered germs and used them to make vaccines.
items.random_wrong: Jenner used penicillin to cure smallpox.
items.why: Jenner worked empirically, not from germ theory which was Pasteur's. Students collapse the discoveries together; vaccination predates the science explaining it.

items.q: Why did Japan attack Pearl Harbor?
items.correct: To cripple the US Pacific Fleet and buy time to secure resource-rich Southeast Asia after the US oil embargo threatened Japanese expansion.
items.misconception: Japan attacked because it wanted to conquer America.
items.random_wrong: Japan attacked to support Germany's invasion of the USSR.
items.why: Japan never planned to conquer the US — the goal was a defensive perimeter. Students assume the attack signalled invasion ambitions when it was a desperate strategic gamble.

items.q: Why did the American colonies rebel against Britain?
items.correct: Disputes over taxation without representation after the Seven Years' War, growing colonial identity, and British attempts to tighten imperial control on previously autonomous colonies.
items.misconception: The colonists rebelled because they were heavily oppressed and impoverished.
items.random_wrong: Because Britain banned them from owning land west of the Atlantic.
items.why: Americans were actually among the wealthiest and least-taxed people in the empire. The revolt was about principle and political control, not material suffering — a key counter-intuitive point.

items.q: When was the People's Republic of China proclaimed?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 1 October 1949. “1 May 1949” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which Mao-era policy of 1958-62 caused a massive famine?
items.why: It's Great Leap Forward. “Hundred Flowers Campaign” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Approximately how many years did the Cultural Revolution last (1966-1976)?
items.why: The real figure is 10. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
